contentBlocks.title: Why convert CSV to JSON?
contentBlocks.html:
CSV (Comma-Separated Values) is a simple tabular format — each row is a record, columns are separated by commas or semicolons. CSV files are generated by Excel, Google Sheets, CRM systems (Salesforce, HubSpot), e-commerce platforms (Shopify, WooCommerce), and databases. The limitation is that CSV cannot represent nested data, types (everything is text), or schema validation.

JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is the standard data exchange format for modern APIs, web applications, and NoSQL databases. It supports nested objects, arrays, numeric types, booleans, and null. Converting CSV to JSON transforms flat tabular data into structures ready for REST APIs, MongoDB, Firebase, React/Vue apps, and automation scripts.

The converter auto-detects column headers (first row) and the separator (comma, semicolon, tab) without configuration. It handles quoted values, newlines within values, and Unicode characters.

All processing happens locally in your browser — nothing is sent to any server. No registration, no limits, no tracking.

contentBlocks.title: How does CSV to JSON conversion work?
contentBlocks.html:
CSV (Comma-Separated Values) stores data as rows of text with values separated by commas or semicolons. JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) organizes the same data as an array of key-value objects, where each row becomes an object and each column header becomes a key.

The converter reads the first row of the CSV file as column headers and uses them as property names in each JSON object. Every subsequent row becomes a separate object in the resulting JSON array. For example, a CSV with columns "name,email,age" produces objects like {"name": "...", "email": "...", "age": "..."}.

The output is properly formatted JSON with indentation for readability. All values are preserved as strings by default. The conversion happens entirely in your browser — no data is sent to any server.

contentBlocks.title: Tips for converting CSV to JSON
contentBlocks.description: A few tips to help you avoid common issues during conversion:

items.title: Column headers
items.description: Make sure your CSV has a header row. The converter uses the first row as JSON property names. Without headers, the output will use generic keys like column_0, column_1.

items.title: Delimiter detection
items.description: The converter automatically detects whether your CSV uses commas, semicolons, or tabs as delimiters. If the result looks wrong, check that your file uses a consistent separator.

items.title: Special characters
items.description: Values containing commas, quotes, or newlines should be enclosed in double quotes in the CSV. The converter handles standard CSV escaping automatically.

items.title: Encoding
items.description: For best results, use UTF-8 encoded CSV files. Files with other encodings (e.g., Windows-1252) may display special characters incorrectly in the JSON output.

contentBlocks.title: Common CSV formatting issues and how to handle them
contentBlocks.html:
CSV files from different sources can have inconsistent formatting. Here are common issues the converter handles:

  • Different separators – commas, semicolons, tabs. European exports from Excel often use semicolons because commas are used as decimal separators.
  • Quoted values – values containing commas or newlines must be wrapped in double quotes. The converter handles this correctly.
  • Unicode and special characters – accented characters, CJK text, and emoji are preserved in the JSON output.
  • Empty values – empty cells are converted to empty strings or null in JSON, depending on context.

If conversion fails, check that your CSV has consistent column counts across all rows and that quoted values use proper double-quote escaping.

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about CSV to JSON conversion

items.question: Is converting CSV to JSON free?
items.answer: Yes, this converter is completely free with no limits. No registration, no watermarks.

items.question: Does the converter upload my files?
items.answer: No. All processing happens locally in your browser. Your files never leave your device.

items.question: Can I convert multiple files at once?
items.answer: Yes, the converter supports batch conversion. Add multiple files and convert them all simultaneously.

items.question: Does the converter work on mobile?
items.answer: Yes, the converter works on any device with a modern web browser, including smartphones and tablets.

items.question: Is converting CSV to JSON safe?
items.answer: Yes. The entire conversion runs locally in your browser. Your data is never sent to any server. When you close the page, all data is automatically cleared from memory.

items.question: Can I use the JSON output in my application?
items.answer: Yes. The output is standard JSON that works with any programming language, database, or API. You can parse it directly in JavaScript, Python, PHP, Java, or any other language that supports JSON.

items.question: What happens to empty values in CSV?
items.answer: Empty cells in CSV are converted to empty strings in JSON. If your CSV has trailing commas or inconsistent column counts, the converter handles them gracefully.
